info.label: What
info.value: Real maintenance actions for the menuki repo, runnable by a human (TUI) or by Claude (menuki exec).

info.label: Safe by default
info.value: status / unreleased / download-stats / run-tests are read-only. cut-release is guarded by confirm=true.

items.name: Status
items.description: Current branch, latest released tag, and how many commits are unreleased.
items.help: Read-only. A quick 'where am I' before deciding whether to cut a release.

action.command:
printf 'branch:        %s
latest tag:    %s
unreleased:    %s commit(s)
' "$(git branch --show-current)" "$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo none)" "$(git rev-list "$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)"..HEAD --count 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"

items.name: Unreleased
items.description: List the commits on main that are not yet in any release tag.
items.help: Exactly what a new release would ship. Read-only. This is the first thing an agent should check before tagging.

items.name: Download stats
items.description: Per-platform download counts across all GitHub releases (the brew-install proxy).
items.help: Read-only. Needs the gh CLI authenticated. Aggregates asset download_count by file name.

items.name: Run tests
items.description: Build and run the full test suite in Release.
items.help: Slower (~1 min). An agent should run this before cutting a release.

items.name: Cut release
items.description: Tag a new version and push it, which triggers the release workflow. Guarded.
items.help: Two typed inputs: version must be vX.Y.Z, and confirm must be true to actually tag/push. With confirm=false it only reports what it would do.

action.command_template: if [ "{confirm}" = "true" ]; then git tag {version} && git push origin {version} && echo "released {version}"; else echo "dry run: would tag and push {version} (set confirm=true to do it)"; fi
